title: Smoky Chewy Mexican Chicken Quesadilla
description: A quick, smoky, and chewy quesadilla featuring tender chicken, cheese, and a splash of Grand Marnier for a subtle citrus kick, all while staying sugarfree.

ingredients.name: flour tortillas
ingredients.notes: or whole‑wheat for a lighter version

instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium heat and drizzle with 1 tsp olive oil.
Add the shredded chicken, smoked paprika, cumin, chili powder, salt, and pepper; cook 2–3 minutes until fragrant.
Stir in Grand Marnier, lime juice, and sugar‑free sweetener; cook another 1 minute. Remove from heat and let rest for 5 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Place two tortillas on the skillet; spread half of the chicken mixture evenly on one tortilla and top with half the cheese and green onions.
Cover with the second tortilla, pressing gently to seal.
Cook 2–3 minutes on each side, until the bottom tortilla is golden brown and the cheese is melted.
Repeat with the remaining tortillas and filling.
Transfer cooked quesadillas to a cutting board, cut into wedges, and serve immediately.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
